content.text: Preferred airlines: United Airlines (Premier 1K) and Alaska Airlines (MVP Gold 75K). Book business class or first class for flights over 90 minutes; economy-plus acceptable for shorter segments. Seat preference: aisle seat, forward cabin when possible. Frequent flyer numbers on file with travel desk (redacted for security).

Preferred chain: Marriott Bonvoy (Platinum Elite). Nightly rate cap per company expense policy: $350. Preference for properties with a fitness center and on-site restaurant. Late check-out requested when available. Non-smoking rooms only.
Uber Black or Uber Comfort preferred for city travel. Rental car (mid-size SUV, Hertz Gold) acceptable when driving between cities. Avoid public transit for client-facing days.

Dietary restriction: gluten-free (celiac). Allergy: shellfish (carries EpiPen). Restaurant selections must accommodate both restrictions. Prefers restaurants that clearly label allergens on their menu.

Early morning meetings (7-8 AM) are acceptable. No meetings after 6:00 PM unless pre-approved by Jordan. Require a minimum 30-minute buffer between meetings in different locations to allow for travel time. Build in at least one 15-minute break each morning and afternoon.

Meals: up to $75/person for client dinners, $50/person otherwise. Ground transport: receipts required over $25. Incidentals: $50/day allowance. All expenses filed in Concur within 5 business days of return.
Executive Assistant: Taylor Reeves, treeves@ourcompany.com, (628) 555-0100. Travel desk (24/7): travel@ourcompany.com, (800) 555-0199. Jordan's mobile: (628) 555-0101.
Prepared for Jordan Chen, Chief Revenue Officer
Prepared by: Taylor Reeves, Executive Assistant
This itinerary covers a four-day, three-city business trip through the U.S. West Coast. The primary objectives are:
•  Deliver keynote address at the SaaS Tech Summit in San Francisco.
•  Conduct Q2 renewal and partnership meetings with key clients in San Francisco and Seattle.
•  Hold internal pipeline reviews with the West Region and PNW teams.
•  Negotiate contract renewal with Columbia Tech Group in Portland.
•  Visit the Portland satellite office for a hiring-plan check-in.
United UA 1542 – Depart: EWR Terminal C 06:00 AM → Arrive: SFO Terminal 3 09:20 AM (nonstop, 5h 20m). Business class, aisle seat 4D. (Assumes Jordan is based on the East Coast or adjust as needed.)
Note: If Jordan departs from a West-Coast home base, this flight is unnecessary. Adjust departure to allow arrival at the first meeting by 8:30 AM.

San Francisco Marriott Marquis, 780 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103. Confirmation: [TBD]. Rate: $329/night (within $350 cap). Check-in from 3:00 PM; luggage drop available on arrival. Bonvoy Platinum late checkout requested.

Breakfast: Hotel restaurant (Marriott Marquis – inform staff of GF/shellfish allergy). Lunch: The Bird, 115 New Montgomery St – extensive gluten-free menu, no shellfish dishes. Dinner: Nopa, 560 Divisadero St – Californian cuisine, well-reviewed GF options, kitchen accommodates allergen requests. Reservation for 1 at 6:30 PM under Chen.
Day 2 – Tuesday, March 24: San Francisco AM / Seattle PM
Alaska AS 306 – Depart: SFO Terminal 2 11:45 AM → Arrive: SEA Gate N 14:00 PM (nonstop, 2h 15m). First class, aisle seat 2C.
Seattle Marriott Waterfront, 2100 Alaskan Way, Seattle, WA 98121. Confirmation: [TBD]. Rate: $339/night (within $350 cap). Check-in from 3:00 PM. Bonvoy Platinum benefits applied.

Breakfast: Marriott Marquis SF – inform staff of allergies. Lunch: Grab-and-go at SFO Terminal 2 (Napa Farms Market has GF wraps). Dinner: Cascina Spinasse, 1531 14th Ave, Seattle – Italian, offers GF pasta, no shellfish on menu. Reservation for 1 at 6:30 PM under Chen.

Breakfast: Marriott Waterfront restaurant (GF/allergy noted on reservation). Lunch: Portage Bay Cafe, 391 Terry Ave N – locally sourced, clearly labeled GF options. Dinner: Eden Hill, 2209 Queen Anne Ave N – small-plates, seasonal, chef accommodates celiac and shellfish allergy. Reservation 6:30 PM under Chen.
Alaska AS 2246 – Depart: SEA Gate D 06:30 AM → Arrive: PDX Concourse D 07:30 AM (nonstop, 1h 00m). Economy Plus (short hop). Hotel checkout 5:30 AM; Uber Black to SEA ~25 min.
United UA 1785 – Depart: PDX Concourse C 16:45 PM → Arrive: SFO Terminal 3 18:50 PM (nonstop, 2h 05m). Business class, aisle seat. (Adjust to final destination as needed.)
Alternative (direct to East Coast): United UA 672 – Depart: PDX 17:20 PM → Arrive: EWR 01:15 AM+1 (nonstop, 4h 55m). Business class.

Breakfast: Proud Mary, 2012 NE Alberta St – Australian-inspired brunch, GF menu. Lunch: Andina, 1314 NW Glisan St – Peruvian, extensive GF options, no shellfish preparations available. Dinner: airport or in-flight (request GF meal if pre-ordered).
•  3 business suits (navy, charcoal, light gray) for client-facing days
•  1 smart-casual outfit for travel day / evening dinner
•  Comfortable shoes for walking between venues
•  Light rain jacket (Pacific NW weather in March)
•  Printed pricing deck for Lumena Tech meeting
•  ROI summary for Pacific Edge annual review
•  EpiPen (shellfish allergy) – carry on person at all times
Use the table below to log expenses during the trip. Submit via Concur within 5 business days of return. Attach all receipts over $25.
Note: Jordan carries an EpiPen for shellfish allergy. In case of allergic reaction, administer EpiPen and call 911 immediately.
